title: Eupatorium iltisii
intro: Eupatorium iltisii, also known as Iltis' Joe-Pye weed or Iltis' boneset, is a perennial plant species in the aster family (Asteraceae) native to the southeastern United States. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it useful for treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Eupatorium iltisii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Eupatorium iltisii is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and interactions.
taxonomy: Eupatorium iltisii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Eupatorium, which consists of about 40 species of plants native to the Americas. The genus Eupatorium is closely related to the genus Eutrochium, and the two are sometimes separated based on the presence or absence of a stalk between the flowers and the leaf. Eupatorium iltisii is native to Mexico and is often found in woodland and rocky areas. This plant is also known to be used in traditional medicine in some parts of Mexico.
